chatbot.title: AI Assistant
chatbot.input_hint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for new line
chatbot.empty_subtitle: Unlike regular chatbots, this AI understands your projects deeply — it can generate visual roadmaps, structured folder trees, and insightful analysis, all ready to save directly to your workspace.
chatbot.card_roadmap_desc: Generate interactive node graphs with dependencies. Pan, zoom, and save them straight to your workspace.
chatbot.card_tree_desc: Get a full project hierarchy — folders, sub-projects, tasks with priorities and deadlines — ready to import.
chatbot.card_analysis_desc: Ask about workload, time spent, bottlenecks, or progress. Get clear insights from your project data.
chatbot.chip_1: Create a roadmap for a mobile app launch
chatbot.chip_2: Build a folder structure for an e-commerce project
chatbot.chip_3: Analyze my team's workload this week
chatbot.chip_4: What tasks are overdue or behind schedule?
chatbot.confirm_desc: All messages will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.

about.hero_subtitle: Manask combines structured task management with Folder → Project → Task hierarchy, Pomodoro timer, AI chatbot, roadmap editor and detailed analytics — all in one web app, no installation needed.
about.feat_desc: From organizing tasks and focusing with Pomodoro, to AI analysis and progress tracking — Manask handles it all.
about.feat1_desc: 3-level hierarchy: Folder → Project → Task. Each task has priority, due date, notes and time spent. Filter, sort and freely drag-and-drop.
about.feat2_desc: 3 modes: Focus / Short Break / Long Break, fully customizable durations. Pick an active task, start the timer, focus without leaving Manask.
about.feat3_desc: 365-day heatmap, Line chart trends by 7d / 30d / 12m, Donut chart task distribution by project, and an overview summary.
about.feat4_desc: AI assistant with workspace context. Generate node-based roadmaps, folder-project-task trees and workload analysis — save directly into the app in one click.
about.feat5_desc: Edit roadmaps on a node-based canvas. Connect milestones, drag to rearrange, customize edge labels. Generate from AI or build from scratch.
about.feat6_desc: IndexedDB caches data locally. Offline requests are queued and auto-synced to the server when connection resumes.
about.feat7_desc: Full bilingual interface in English / Vietnamese. Instant switch from the sidebar, auto-saved per account.
about.feat8_desc: Secure sign-up and login. All folders, projects and tasks are stored server-side and synced per account.
about.team_desc: Three students with one shared goal: making work management truly organized.
about.team_member1_bio: Responsible for designing and building the entire user interface — from layout and animations to smooth interactive experience.
about.team_member2_bio: Builds the server system, RESTful API and database — ensuring every feature runs reliably, securely and efficiently.
about.team_member3_bio: Owns the entire visual direction and user experience — from UX research and wireframes to a consistent design system.
about.comp_quote: "Manask is built to enter the city-level contest organized under the direction of the Hanoi People's Committee — an annual intellectual playground that sparks creative thinking and nurtures the future inventors of the capital."
about.footer_contest: Entered in the Hanoi Youth Creativity Contest · 22nd Edition

help.q_start_1: How do I get started with Manask?
help.a_start_1: Manask organises work in 3 levels: Folder → Project → Task. Open the folder panel on the right, click “Create new” to add a Folder, then create a Project inside the Folder, and finally add Tasks to the Project. That's the basic flow for managing all of your work.
help.q_start_2: What's the difference between a Folder and a Project?
help.a_start_2: A Folder is a broad grouping by area — for example: “Study”, “Work”, “Personal”. A Project is a specific initiative inside a Folder — for example: “IELTS prep”, “Build a website”. Tasks are attached directly to Projects, not to Folders.
help.q_start_3: How do I create a new account?
help.a_start_3: Visit the Sign up page, fill in your full name, email and password, then confirm. Once registered, you can sign in and start using Manask right away.
help.a_start_4: Yes. The Manask UI is responsive and works well on mobile browsers (Safari, Chrome). No app install needed — just open the URL.
help.q_start_5: Will my data be lost if I close the browser or switch devices?
help.a_start_5: No. All data is saved on the server and tied to your account. Sign back in on any device and you'll see all your Folders, Projects and Tasks. Manask also uses IndexedDB to keep data available locally during short connection drops.
help.a_start_6: Partially. While offline, you can still work as usual — changes are queued locally and automatically synced to the server once the connection is restored.
help.q_tasks_1: How do I create my first task?
help.a_tasks_1: Open the folder panel on the right and pick a Project → click “New task” at the top right of the task area → type the task name and press Enter or click confirm. The new task shows up in the list immediately.
help.q_tasks_2: Can I set priority on a task?
help.a_tasks_2: Yes. Click a task to open Task Detail → choose Priority: Low / Medium / High. The task is colour-coded in the list so it's easy to spot and prioritise.
help.q_tasks_3: Can I set a deadline for a task?
help.a_tasks_3: Yes. In Task Detail, set the Due date field. You can also ask the Chatbot which tasks are due soon or overdue.
help.q_tasks_4: Can I drag and drop tasks to reorder?
help.a_tasks_4: Yes. Hold and drag a task to the position you want within the same Project. The new order is saved automatically — no extra confirmation needed.
help.q_tasks_5: How do Filter and Sort work?
help.a_tasks_5: Click Filter or Sort on the toolbar above the task list → choose conditions (priority, deadline, status...) → click Apply. You can combine multiple conditions to narrow down the list.
help.q_tasks_6: Where does Time Spent come from?
help.a_tasks_6: Time Spent is automatically accumulated from completed Pomodoro sessions linked to that task. To track time accurately, select the right task before starting a Pomodoro — each completed Focus session adds to the task's Time Spent.
help.q_tasks_7: Can I restore a deleted task?
help.a_tasks_7: Manask doesn't have a trash or undo for deleted tasks yet. Deletion is permanent — please confirm carefully before deleting.
help.q_pomo_1: What's the standard Pomodoro workflow in Manask?
help.a_pomo_1: Pick the task to focus on → click Start to begin a Focus session (25 minutes by default) → when it ends, take a Short Break (5 minutes) → repeat 4 cycles → after 4 Focus sessions, take a Long Break (15 minutes). The timer switches modes automatically after each session.
help.q_pomo_2: Can I customise Focus / Break durations?
help.a_pomo_2: Yes. Click the Settings icon on the Pomodoro page → adjust Focus, Short Break and Long Break durations. Settings are saved automatically and apply to subsequent sessions.
help.q_pomo_3: Do I have to select a task before pressing Start?
help.a_pomo_3: It's not required — Start works without a selected task. However, to log focus time into the task's Time Spent, you should pick the task first.
help.q_pomo_4: Can I pause or reset the timer?
help.a_pomo_4: Yes. While the timer is running, click Pause to pause and Resume to continue. Click Reset to return to the start of the current session.
help.q_pomo_5: Can I enable a sound notification when time's up?
help.a_pomo_5: Yes. Open Settings on the Pomodoro page to enable sound. Manask doesn't support choosing custom sounds yet — only the default sound is used.
help.q_chat_1: What can the AI Chatbot do?
help.a_chat_1: The AI can: generate a node-based Roadmap for any topic, create a complete Folder-Project-Task tree, analyse your current workload, and answer questions about your workspace. It's given project context so suggestions match your actual situation.
help.q_chat_2: Does the AI read my task data?
help.a_chat_2: Yes. The AI is granted access to your workspace context (Folders, Projects, Tasks) during the chat session for more accurate analysis and suggestions. This data is only used within the chat session and is not shared outside the system.
help.q_chat_3: After the AI builds a folder tree, how do I save it to my workspace?
help.a_chat_3: When the AI returns a Folder-Project-Task structure or a Roadmap, a preview panel appears on the right. Click “Save to workspace” in that panel — the data is added directly to your Dashboard or Roadmap Editor.
help.a_chat_4: Yes. Chat history is stored on the server and persists across sessions, but only the most recent sessions are kept — not the entire history. To clear it, click “Clear history” in the chat UI. Note: this can't be undone.
help.a_chat_5: Yes. The AI supports both Vietnamese and English. Ask questions or give instructions in whichever language is more comfortable.
help.q_road_1: How do I create a new node on the canvas?
help.a_road_1: Drag a node from the folder tree on the right onto the canvas to create a new node.
help.q_road_2: How do I connect two nodes?
help.a_road_2: Hover over the edge of the source node until a connection dot appears → click and drag to the target node. The connection line is drawn automatically. You can add a label to the connection by clicking on it.
help.q_road_3: Can I delete a node or connection?
help.a_road_3: Yes. Select the node or connection → press Delete or Backspace, or use the delete option in the context menu (right-click on the element).
help.q_road_4: Can I generate a Roadmap with AI?
help.a_road_4: Yes. Go to AI Chat → ask the AI to generate a Roadmap for a specific topic or goal → the AI returns a node-based diagram in the preview panel → click “Save to workspace” to send it to the Roadmap Editor for further editing.
help.a_road_5: Partially. While offline, the canvas still displays normally and you can keep editing. Changes are queued offline and automatically synced to the server when the connection is restored.
help.a_stats_1: The heatmap shows tasks completed and focus hours per day across the last 365 days. Darker cells mean more tasks completed and more focus hours that day. Hover any cell to see exact numbers.
help.q_stats_2: What does the donut chart show?
help.a_stats_2: The donut chart shows how tasks are distributed across each Project in the selected period. Each colour represents a Project, helping you quickly spot which Project is taking up the most work.
help.q_stats_3: What do the line chart and bar chart track?
help.a_stats_3: The line chart shows the trend of completed tasks over time in the selected period. The bar chart shows on-time completion rate per Project — on-time and late portions are stacked so you can compare across projects.
help.q_stats_4: How are the “Week / Month / Year” periods calculated?
help.a_stats_4: All are relative to today: Week = last 7 days · Month = last 30 days · Year = last 12 months. They are not calendar-aligned (not from the start of the month or year).
help.q_stats_5: Are statistics updated in real time?
help.a_stats_5: Data updates when you reload the page or switch the period. Changes such as task completion show up in Statistics on the next load.
help.q_acc_1: Where do I change my display name?
help.a_acc_1: Click the account area at the bottom of the sidebar (Manask logo + name + email) → the Profile popup appears → edit your display name → click Save. The change applies immediately across the whole UI.
help.q_acc_2: How do I change my password?
help.a_acc_2: Click the account area at the bottom of the sidebar → Profile popup → choose Change password → enter your current and new password → confirm. The new password takes effect right after saving.
help.q_acc_3: What if I forget my password?
help.a_acc_3: Manask doesn't support email-based password reset yet. If you forget your password, please contact the development team for help recovering your account.
help.a_acc_4: Yes. All changes are saved on the server per account. Sign in on any device or browser and you'll see the same data.
help.a_acc_5: Click the account area at the bottom of the sidebar → Profile popup → click Sign out. The session ends and you're returned to the sign-in page.
